documentation:
# Buildgrid Metering Service Client

🚧 This library is currently WIP and the interfaces might not be stable while the version below `1.0.0`. 🚧

Asyncio Python client of [`buildgrid-metering`](https://gitlab.com/BuildGrid/buildgrid-metering) service.

## Example

Suppose `buildgrid-metering` is running on `http://localhost:8000` requiring no authentication.
The service can be queried using this library via its `asyncio` interfaces.

```python
import asyncio
from buildgrid_metering.client import MeteringServiceClient
from buildgrid_metering.client.auth import (
    AuthTokenConfig,
    AuthTokenLoader,
    AuthTokenMode,
)
from buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses import ComputingUsage, Identity, Usage

token_loader = AuthTokenLoader(AuthTokenConfig(AuthTokenMode.NONE, ""))
client = MeteringServiceClient("http://localhost:8000", token_loader)
identity = Identity(instance="dev", workflow="build", actor="tool", subject="username")


async def main():
    # Put usage of identity
    usage = Usage(computing=ComputingUsage(utime=1, stime=2, maxrss=3))
    await client.put_usage(identity, "op", usage)

    # Check throttling of identity
    resp = await client.get_throttling(identity)
    print(resp)


if __name__ == "__main__":\
    # Run the script with asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

```

## Contact us

See more details at [BuildGrid Resources](https://buildgrid.build/user/about.html#resources).

## Contributing

See more details at [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) and [BuildGrid](https://buildgrid.build/developer/contributing.html).

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.api.GetThrottlingResponse
documentation:
Summary if an identity should be throttled for the next remote execution

Args:
    throttled (bool): if an identity should be throttled for the next remote execution
    tracked_usage (Usage): A subset of resource usage that is over the threshold
    tracked_time_window_secs: If set, this is the time window in seconds
        when the identity over consumed resource usage

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.api.PutUsageRequest
documentation:
Put the resource usage of an identity

Args:
    operation_name (str): REAPI operation name
    usage (Usage): resource usage

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.max_attempts
documentation: Max attempts to retry the API call. Default to 0 (no retry).

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.exp_base
documentation: Exponential base before retry. Default to 1.5

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.multiplier
documentation: Multiplier for exponential back-off. Defaults to 1.0

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.max_wait
documentation: Maximum of wait time in seconds between retries. Default to 30.0

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.http_statuses
documentation: HTTP status codes to retry, e.g. 503. Default to empty.

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.exception_types
documentation: Exception types to retry. Default to empty.

class_variables.name: buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig.cause_exception_types
documentation: Cause exception types to retry. Default to empty.
RetryConfig for MeteringServiceClient

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.Identity
documentation:
Identity that consumes build resource

Args:
    Instance: REAPI service instance, e.g., "dev"
    Workflow: The associated workflow of the operation, e.g., "build"
    Actor: The tooling or agent that submitted the operation, e.g., "my-build-tool"
    Subject: The end user that submitted the operation, e.g. "user1"

methods.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.ResourceUsage.combine
documentation: Combine two resource usages of the same type

methods.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.ResourceUsage.get_throttled
documentation:
Get a subset of this resource type that exceeds the predefined threshold
while attributes that are <= threshold are default-valued.

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.NumericResourceUsage
documentation:
A subtype of ResourceUsage where each attribute is either int or float.
It provides reasonable default methods.

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.ComputingUsage
documentation: Basic computing resource usage defined in getrusage(2)

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.CASUsage
documentation: IO usage of ContentAddressableStorage

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.RPCUsage
documentation: Counters of RPC invocations

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.models.dataclasses.Usage
documentation: The aggregation of all kinds of usages

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.client.auth.AuthTokenConfig
documentation:
Config on how to load the JWT token.
if `mode == CONSTANT`, the token will always be the `value`
if `mode == FILEPATH`, it reads the file located at `value`
if `mode == NONE`, it returns `None`

classes.name: buildgrid_metering.client.auth.AuthTokenLoader

methods.name: buildgrid_metering.client.auth.AuthTokenLoader.get_token
documentation:
Get the JWT authentication token

Raises:
    ValueError: if the `mode` is not supported

Returns:
    str | None: JWT if `mode` is not NONE
A class that loads JWT authentication token based on the config

methods.name: buildgrid_metering.client.client._MeteringServiceClient.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the client

Args:
    base_url (str): base URL of the service,
        e.g. http://localhost:8000 or https://somedomain.com
    token_loader (AuthTokenLoader): token loader
    request_timeout (float, optional): HTTP Request timeout . Defaults to 5.0.
    headers (Optional[dict[str, str]], optional): default headers.
        Defaults to None.
    retry_configs: See `buildgrid_metering.client.retry.RetryConfig`
        for details and default values.

documentation: Python client of BuildGrid Metering Service
